g.text: match field names to kernel offsets

text.tspan: // CO-RE read - offset resolved at load!
// libbpf patches correct offset at load
// CO-RE read - offset resolved at load!
// libbpf patches correct offset at load

div.p: Key Insight: With CO-RE, you compile once and the bytecode contains field names, not offsets. At load time, libbpf reads your kernel's BTF, finds the actual offset of i_size in struct inode, and patches the bytecode. Same .o file works on any kernel with BTF support.
